qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via Vertex AI location parameter host injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes SQL injection in proxy API key verification where caller-supplied key values are mixed into database query text. This finding describes SSRF via Vertex AI location parameter injection in URL construction. Different vulnerability class (SSRF vs SQL injection), different affected component (Vertex AI pass-through endpoints vs proxy API key verification), different root cause (URL host interpolation vs database query parameterization).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SQL injection vulnerability in proxy API key verification. The advisory describes a database query that mixes caller-supplied API key values into query text instead of using parameterized queries, affecting the proxy API key verification path. Neither qualifying finding involves SQL injection, database queries, or the API key verification component. The ssrf-vertex-ai-location-injection finding concerns SSRF via Vertex AI location parameter injection in URL construction. The example finding concerns deserialization RCE in an import endpoint. Both are different vulnerability classes affecting different components with different root causes.
evidence: Advisory describes SQL injection (CWE-89) in proxy API key verification
Advisory root cause: caller-supplied key value mixed into database query text instead of parameterized query
Advisory affected component: proxy API key checks via Authorization header on LLM API routes
ssrf-vertex-ai-location-injection: SSRF vulnerability in Vertex AI pass-through endpoints via location parameter injection
example: Deserialization RCE vulnerability in import endpoint workflow
No finding mentions SQL injection, database queries, or API key verification logic
